channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Furniture dumped, 18th June
item.description:
General furniture, been there for ages.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Vauban Street, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Next to bike shed on Donaldson Road and Brondesbury Road, 18th June
item.description:
More rubbish and fly tipping . Please pick up

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brondesbury Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Couple of doors dumped, 18th June
item.description:
Doors left by recycling bins

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Vauban Street, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tip, 18th June
item.description:
Mattresses, bed frames, other household items. There is also a mattress leaning against the wall on furzby road. Thanks
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kyverdale Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 18th June
item.description:
There is persistent fly tipping on this corner and this time a dispersed litter bag please fix and put up signs

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Balchen Road, Kidbrooke
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping at East Road bus stop C, 18th June
item.description:
There is a large amount of fly tipped rubbish at bus stop C, East Road Welling. It has increased overnight as there was a single bag there yesterday.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Upper Wickham Lane, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped rubbish, 18th June
item.description:
Dumped Rubbish still not picked up from yesterday that i had reported.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 226, Newlands Park, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown trees/bushes, 18th June
item.description:
Over growth causing footpath users to walk at edge of footpath

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bexley Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish & overgrowth, 18th June
item.description:
Lots of rubbish and vastly overgrown trees and bushes

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Nuthatch Gardens, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish, 18th June
item.description:
On grass rear of 136 wren path se280dy gardeners cut grass around it

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Wren Path, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: High volume of rubbish and problem still not resolved, 18th June
item.description:
It’s back again and more. As a person living in the area I would like to see this resolved at the root cause. Clearly picking it up doesn’t fix the problem as it’s back the week after. Why not have commercial bins for example?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southwark Park Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree, 18th June
item.description:
Tree need badly cut back going to poke someone in there eyes

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 144, Croydon Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown madness!, 18th June
item.description:
Its getting beyond a joke just how overgrown tbe Watermeadows Open Space is now. Our dog has been injured as a direct result (grass seed in ear, nettles embedded in paws, brambles catching fur). Why is this not being maintained? Its becoming a breeding ground for litter, antisocial behaviour, and its just an absolute eyesore. Im sure many other members of the public that use this park agree. Growing tired of it now. Just utter laziness.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brook Way, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti in Millfields park, 18th June
item.description:
There is graffiti on the path and bench in the park - close to Lea Bridge Road on the diagonal path.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lea Bridge Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sign broken and fallen onto road, 18th June
item.description:
Very busy main junction, crossing also very busy so traffic needs to be clearly signposted. Sign broken, has been for several weeks if not months. Needs replaced please.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 383, Lordship Lane, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken bench Bt bus stop, 18th June
item.description:
Bolts have been removed making the bench unstable and the wooden slats are hanging making it dangerous
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 67, Avery Hill Road, New Eltham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Stop line, pedestrians stud, zigzag faded on both approaches- required repainting, 18th June

Stop line, pedestrians stud, zigzag faded on both approaches- required repainting
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Essex Road, Islington, Greater London

Stop line, pedestrians stud, zigzag faded on both approaches- required repainting

item.title: In Shortlands Rd, Shortlands about 20m SW of the traffic signal jct with Bromley Rd (opposite side idmf road from the station) both the pole-mounted litter bin and the freestanding boxy recycling bibs are full and overflowing rubbish onto the public footway., 18th June
item.description:
In Shortlands Rd, Shortlands about 20m SW of the traffic signal jct with Bromley Rd (opposite side idmf road from the station) both the pole-mounted litter bin and the freestanding boxy recycling bibs are full and overflowing rubbish onto the public footway. This has been like this for at least 3 days
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Shortlands Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
